films.title: The Murder Ballad of Hollow Pine
films.synopsis: Disgraced detective Cyrus Vane—stripped of his badge for "hearing confessions that weren't there"—drifts into Hollow Pine, a fog-drowned mountain town where every resident answers questions only by singing. As townsfolk vanish one by one, Cyrus realizes the killer leaves clues exclusively in the lyrics of a haunting folk ballad nobody admits writing. To solve it he must learn the song's final unsung verse—but anyone who completes the melody dies. Part toe-tapping hootenanny, part slow-dread nightmare, it's a whodunit where the truth harmonizes back.
films.pitch: A Disgraced Detective stumbles into a Indie Folk Musical conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Psychological Horror.

films.title: Murder Ballad: A Toe-Tapping Investigation
films.synopsis: Banjo-strumming folk troubadour Earl "Two-Capo" Dabney returns to tiny Possum Hollow to headline the Heritage Pickle Festival, only to discover the festival's prize-winning gourd has been STOLEN — and the local true-crime podcast is already framing him for it. To clear his name before the town goes bankrupt, Earl must out-sleuth a deadpan documentary crew, a fast-talking ex-fiancée who runs the sheriff's office, and his own incurable habit of narrating crimes in three-part harmony. Every clue becomes a verse. Every suspect gets a chorus. Possum Hollow has eleven hours and one tambourine to save itself.
films.pitch: Set in a world where True Crime and Screwball coexist uneasily, our hero — a Folk Musician — must save... something. Probably a town.

films.title: Saucier: A Detective's Reduction
films.synopsis: Expelled from the Brackmoor Academy of Applied Sorcery after his evidence-summoning spell accidentally vaporized the dean, ex-Detective Inspector Roland Pim now hosts a streaming food documentary nobody asked for. Armed with a cracked scrying mirror and a microwave he insists is "artisanal," Roland tries to crack the cold case of who poisoned the realm's last great soufflé — while ugly-crying over a frozen shepherd's pie. Each episode he interviews enchanted ingredients that refuse to confess. It's a whodunit, a cookery show, and a breakdown, plated beautifully.
films.pitch: Three-act structure: Magical Academia in act one, Food Doc by act three, and a Disgraced Detective sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.

films.title: The Last Confession of Sam Spadehands
films.synopsis: Father Augustine "Gus" Malone hasn't believed in anything since the diocese replaced the choir with a synergy app. So when the parish bingo cash vanishes and a stained-glass saint turns up shattered, he hangs a trench coat over his cassock and starts working the case like the gumshoe God never called him to be. The trail of lies runs from the bishop's brunch to a megachurch with valet parking, and every suspect wants absolution before an alibi. It's a hardboiled whodunit where the only thing more corrupt than the killer is the parking-lot expansion committee.
films.pitch: It's Satire meets Hardboiled Detective... but the protagonist is a Disillusioned Priest.

films.title: The Made Man Who Never Was
films.synopsis: Sal "Knuckles" Provolone has spent twenty years breaking kneecaps for the Carnevale family — until he notices his apartment has no doors, his wife answers to three different names, and the same pigeon keeps landing on the same ledge at 3:14 PM exactly. Sal slowly realizes his entire "life" is an elaborate long con staged inside his own skull to crack the location of a vault only his subconscious remembers. To survive the unraveling, the enforcer must out-think the architects haunting his memory — before the heist finishes itself and erases him for good. A claustrophobic descent where the only thing scarier than the crew robbing him is what they're robbing from.
films.pitch: Logline: When a Mob Enforcer discovers their quiet life is actually a Heist, they must master the genre conventions of Psychological Horror to survive.

films.title: Till Death Do Us Part: The Eternal Embrace Exposé
films.synopsis: Mortician Desmond Quill has buried half the county and married none of it — until a documentary crew catches him signing a hasty marriage license to inherit his late mentor's funeral home. The catch: his "bride," Posy, is the recruiter for the Eternal Embrace, a wellness commune that worships embalming fluid and matching beige jumpsuits. As the cameras roll, Desmond must fake honeymoon bliss while smuggling out evidence that the cult's "afterlife retreats" are suspiciously one-way. It's a slow-burn marriage of convenience wrapped in a true-crime takedown, and someone keeps rearranging the caskets.
films.pitch: Cold open: a Undertaker, alone, in the rain. The genre? Cult Exposé. The vibe? Unmistakably Marriage of Convenience. Cue title card.

films.title: Crystal Ball Evidence: A Murder Foretold
films.synopsis: Madame Velveteen Crow runs the only psychic-hotline-slash-crime-lab in the rust-belt town of Cinder Hollow, where she's legally required to sing every prophecy in full power-ballad. When the mayor turns up dead — and her tarot deck names the killer three days BEFORE the body cools — the skeptical state coroner demands chain-of-custody on a vision. Now Velveteen must reconcile lab results with séance results before a wailing electric-guitar prophecy comes true and the whole town goes up in pyrotechnic flame. It's spectral evidence versus spectrographs, scored to a 110-decibel organ solo.
films.pitch: Set in a world where Forensic Procedural and Rock Opera coexist uneasily, our hero — a Fortune Teller — must save... something. Probably a town.

films.title: Encore at the End of the World
films.synopsis: Burnout glam-rocker Dex Mallory hasn't had a hit since 1994, so when a slick "wellness label" offers him a comeback tour, he signs without reading the fine print. Turns out the label is a deep-cover surveillance front — and somewhere between the laser-tag-themed safe house and a microwave lasagna, Dex realizes the tour is recruiting fans into a doomsday cult that worships HIM. Now armed with a wireless headset mic and the worst hangover in espionage history, Dex must out-spy his handlers and de-program his own fan club before the encore literally ends civilization. A thriller about reverb, redemption, and reading contracts.
films.pitch: Three-act structure: Spy Thriller in act one, Cult Exposé by act three, and a Washed-Up Rock Star sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.

films.title: Footnotes: The Musical Truth
films.synopsis: Reclusive literary genius Bartholomew Quill hasn't left his crumbling Vermont farmhouse — or finished his second novel — in nineteen years. So a desperate documentary crew moves in to film "the writing process," only to discover that Bartholomew can no longer speak in prose: every confession, panic attack, and grocery list now erupts as a fully choreographed Broadway dance number. As the town of Maple Hollow faces bankruptcy without his long-promised bestseller, the camera rolls while Bartholomew tap-dances his way toward one terrifying final chapter. It's a tell-all about a man who can only tell it in jazz hands.
films.pitch: Set in a world where Mockumentary and Dance Musical coexist uneasily, our hero — a Reclusive Author — must save... something. Probably a town.

films.title: The Last Verse at Loon Lake
films.synopsis: When folk darling Marigold Pike returns to the sleepy lakeside hamlet of Loon's Rest for a charity hootenanny, the only thing duller than her ex's banjo solo is the weather report — until the dam upriver starts groaning. Then the festival's beloved fiddler turns up drowned in three inches of suspiciously rising water. With the valley flooding by the hour and twelve sweater-clad suspects trapped in a tilting lodge, Marigold must solve the murder before the whole town goes under — all while resisting the smoldering park ranger who keeps shouting evacuation orders directly into her soul. Cozy, doomed, and unbearably romantic.
films.pitch: Pitch: Disaster x Cozy Whodunit. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Folk Musician.

films.title: The Gentleman Swindler's Awakening
films.synopsis: Seventeen-year-old Percival "Pip" Hargrove is the finest forger of love-letters in all of Brighton, fleecing lovesick aristocrats one counterfeit sonnet at a time. But when he's hired to ghostwrite a courtship for the dazzling, sharp-tongued Lady Arabella — who immediately sees through every con — Pip discovers the one swindle he can't run is on his own heart. As his lies snowball into a season-long social catastrophe, he must choose between the perfect grift and his first, terrifyingly genuine feeling. A bittersweet drawing-room comedy about growing up, falling hard, and finally telling the truth.
films.pitch: Picture a Coming-of-Age setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Period Romance. Our reluctant hero: a Con Artist.

films.title: The Bachelor Always Rings Twice
films.synopsis: Reformed grifter Sully Marchetti talks his way onto a primetime dating show using a fake billionaire backstory — only to discover that the franchise's new producer is Delphine, the woman he conned and abandoned at an airport seven years ago. Now she controls every rose ceremony, every confessional cut, and every chance he has to win back the one mark he never wanted to leave. As the cameras roll, Sully has to run the longest con of his life: telling the truth. Equal parts elimination chaos and aching second-chance romance, it's the most honest lie ever broadcast.
films.pitch: Cold open: a Con Artist, alone, in the rain. The genre? Reality Competition. The vibe? Unmistakably Second-Chance Romance. Cue title card.

films.title: Commander-in-Grief: The Casserole Conspiracy
films.synopsis: Senator Roland Pike spent twelve years building the perfect campaign — until a botched black-ops raid he secretly authorized goes catastrophically sideways, killing his only loyal aide. Now hunted, disgraced, and weeping into a 99-cent Salisbury steak every single night, Roland reinvents himself as an amateur detective to clear his name and unmask the shadow donor who set him up. Armed with a corkboard, a laser pointer, and zero forensic training, he stumbles from gunfights to garden-party interrogations. A whisper-thriller about ambition, grief, and the precise reheating time of frozen lasagna.
films.pitch: Three-act structure: Military Action in act one, Amateur Sleuth by act three, and a Ambitious Politician sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.

films.title: Blade & Blaster: The Two-Bit Heist of Dagmar Vex
films.synopsis: Dagmar Vex is a small-time crook who fences cursed amulets out of a cargo hold and has never once paid for parking his hover-mule. When he accidentally swipes a sword that's also a sentient plasma rifle — and it won't stop narrating his crimes — every wizard-bounty-hunter in the Nine Star-Holds wants his head. Now Dagmar has eleven hours to return the blade, dodge a dragon that files taxes, and save the only frontier town that still lets him run a tab. He is spectacularly unqualified. The sword agrees.
films.pitch: Set in a world where Space Opera and Sword & Sorcery coexist uneasily, our hero — a Small-Time Crook — must save... something. Probably a town.

films.title: The Rapture Index
films.synopsis: Father Elias Crane lost his faith somewhere between the third funeral and his second bottle of communion wine. So when a basement archive in the Vatican reveals that an extinction-level solar flare was predicted — and deliberately concealed — by a shadow conclave, he's the only man unholy enough to chase the truth. With twelve hours until the sky catches fire, Elias races a smoldering cabal, a seismologist who tempts him toward the very vows he abandoned, and his own creeping certainty that maybe the world deserves it. Salvation is classified. Damnation is right on schedule.
films.pitch: Pitch: Disaster x Conspiracy Thriller. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Disillusioned Priest.

films.title: Whodunnit, Reverb?
films.synopsis: Once the frontman of stadium gods Velvet Hernia, Dax Marrow now lives in a leaking lake house where the furniture occasionally floats and his goldfish gives advice. When his last surviving bandmate is found drowned in a hot tub full of his own platinum records, the police rule it "a vibe" — so Dax, armed with three working brain cells and a karaoke machine, appoints himself detective. Between sobbing into lukewarm microwave lasagna and interrogating a suspiciously articulate roadie, he discovers the truth was hidden in a B-side all along. The reverb knows everything.
films.pitch: Three-act structure: Surreal Comedy in act one, Amateur Sleuth by act three, and a Washed-Up Rock Star sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.

films.title: Hosanna in the Highest Conspiracy
films.synopsis: Father Lucian Voss lost his faith, found a corkboard, and never looked back. In this "documentary," the chain-smoking, dangerously handsome priest croons torch-song confessionals about the Vatican's secret thermostat cabal while a trembling investigative cellist follows him through candlelit catacombs. Every revelation is sung. Every duet ends in tears or sin. As Lucian inches toward the truth — and toward his cameraman, Brother Diego — the choir gets louder, the lighting gets steamier, and somebody's clearly going to die before the third act key change.
films.pitch: Pitch: Conspiracy Doc x Tragic Musical. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Disillusioned Priest.

films.title: The Honorable Affair
films.synopsis: Newly elected Senator Cassius Thorne discovers his predecessor was murdered to bury a treasonous ledger — and the only witnesses are the powdered aristocrats of a 1814 estate frozen in time by their own scandal-bound etiquette. To extract the truth, Cassius must abandon backroom horse-trading and master the lethal arts of the calling card, the chaperoned waltz, and the devastatingly worded letter. Every confession requires a courtship; every alliance demands he risk his heart to the icy Lady Ravensmere, who may be both his killer and his soulmate. In a world where a misplaced glove ends careers, the most dangerous campaign is romance.
films.pitch: A Ambitious Politician stumbles into a Prestige Drama conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Period Romance.

films.title: The Chosen Three: Eliminated With Extreme Prejudice
films.synopsis: Dale Prunch, a mild-mannered parking enforcement officer, is informed by an ancient prophecy (and a very enthusiastic man named Gary) that he alone can stop a citywide conspiracy. The catch: the cult's secrets are sealed by reality-TV logic, so Dale must survive themed elimination challenges, confessional-booth betrayals, and a "synergy round" with his accidental best friend, a disgraced former game-show host. Together they bicker, form an alliance, and lie to a confessional camera nobody can find. Saving the world means outlasting the other contestants — and surviving the dramatic reveal music.
films.pitch: A Reluctant Chosen One stumbles into a Buddy Comedy conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Reality Competition.

films.title: The Quiet Game: A Tournament of Hands
films.synopsis: Vincent "The Whisper" Kovac retired from contract killing to dominate the world's most prestigious competitive thumb-wrestling circuit — and a reverent documentary crew has captured every dread-soaked, inexplicably sweaty moment. As rival champions vanish under suspicious circumstances, talking-head interviews dissolve into fever dreams of slow-motion knuckle-cracking and a referee who may be Death itself in a polo shirt. Vincent insists he's a changed man, yet his thumb still knows 14 lethal pressure points. It's the feel-good sports story of the year, if your feelings are mostly unease and longing.
films.pitch: Pitch: Sports Doc x Surreal Comedy. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Retired Assassin.

films.title: Hostile Tides: A First Contact Romance
films.synopsis: Captain Eli "Barnacle" Rourke has hated exactly two things his whole life: paperwork and anything smarter than him. So when a luminous squid-shaped alien named Vessel-7 crash-lands its cathedral-sized ship in his fishing lanes and demands he stop "polluting the broadcast frequency with bait smell," it's loathing at first sight. But as the two trade insults across a translation buoy and accidentally save each other from a kraken-class warship, Rourke realizes the most terrifying first contact isn't with the cosmos — it's with feelings. Humanity's fate now hinges on whether one stubborn old sailor can apologize.
films.pitch: Picture a First Contact setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Enemies-to-Lovers. Our reluctant hero: a Grizzled Sea Captain.

films.title: Landslide: A Comedy of Tumbleweeds
films.synopsis: State Senator Dale Brisco has a five-year plan, a fake folksy accent, and a campaign bus that just exploded outside a town called Consequence. Stranded in the dusty middle of nowhere with a runaway intern, a stolen prize bull, and a rival candidate who keeps showing up in increasingly improbable disguises, Dale's slick fast-talking screwball charm slowly erodes into squinting, sweaty Neo-Western reckoning. To win the swing district he never asked to be stuck in, he must outdraw a sheriff, woo a diner waitress with the only honest soul in the county, and stop crying into gas-station lasagna. Democracy has never been this hot, this dumb, or this dehydrated.
films.pitch: Three-act structure: Screwball in act one, Neo-Western by act three, and a Ambitious Politician sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.

films.title: Madame Pustula's Inevitable Forecast
films.synopsis: Madame Pustula, a small-town fortune teller with a tragic addiction to slamming doors and hiding lovers in armoires, foresees one tiny detail in every client's future: the exact hour their flesh begins to rearrange itself. When a misdelivered prophecy causes the mayor, the vicar, and three identical insurance salesmen to start sprouting extra elbows during a single chaotic dinner party, Pustula must keep the bursting boils a polite secret. Doors slam, organs migrate, and nobody acknowledges the screaming. A farce so well-timed it's practically gangrenous.
films.pitch: Cold open: a Fortune Teller, alone, in the rain. The genre? Farce. The vibe? Unmistakably Body Horror. Cue title card.

films.title: The Ballad of Real Things
films.synopsis: Wendell Crane, a soft-spoken folk musician with a banjo and a recurring dream about his own knees, slowly realizes his entire life has been a brooding prestige drama — long silences, meaningful staring, a wife who keeps "discovering things about herself." To escape the crushing weight of all that meaning, Wendell must learn the rules of Surreal Comedy: befriending a sentient harmonica, accepting that his porch is now in France, and performing a breakup song to a jury of identical men named Gary. If he can't make the universe laugh, the third act will make him cry. He has chosen the harmonica.
films.pitch: Logline: When a Folk Musician discovers their quiet life is actually a Psychological Drama, they must master the genre conventions of Surreal Comedy to survive.

films.title: The Vanishing Act of Silas Crow
films.synopsis: Silas Crow, a third-rate vaudeville magician with a flask of mescaline and a guilty conscience, gets stranded in the cursed mining town of Hollow Mercy, where the sun never quite sets and everyone he's ever wronged seems to be living. When the local sheriff is sawn in half — for real — Silas realizes the only way out is to confront the buried trauma he keeps hidden up his sleeve. Part hallucinatory gunslinger fever dream, part slow-burn therapy session at high noon, this is a Western where the deadliest weapon is honesty. Pick a card. Any card. They're all the same card. It's you.
films.pitch: A Stage Magician stumbles into a Acid Western conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Psychological Drama.

films.title: The Comeback Skin
films.synopsis: Washed-up action legend Rance Delacroix hasn't headlined a hit since the '90s, so when a boutique "wellness studio" offers a miracle anti-aging treatment, he says yes before reading the waiver. The cream works terrifyingly well — his face de-wrinkles, his abs return, and his old skin starts peeling off into a fully sentient, scheming younger clone who wants his career, his agent, and his Malibu deck. Now Rance must out-deadpan a conspiracy of beautiful body-horror doppelgängers while literally holding himself together. A grisly, deadpan comedy about vanity, sequels, and never reading the fine print.
films.pitch: A Aging Movie Star stumbles into a Dark Comedy conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Body Horror.

films.title: The Mating Season of Agent Vesper
films.synopsis: Retired superspy Vesper Cole has traded dead drops for a cliffside puffin sanctuary, narrating the secret lives of seabirds for a wildlife streaming network. Her cover is going perfectly — until the BBC-style documentary crew sends a velvet-voiced narrator, Lars, who turns out to be the enemy handler she once left at the altar. Now, as a hidden cartel hunts them both across the breeding colony, Vesper must protect her flock, decode a microfilm sewn into a nesting gull, and resist the one man her old agency forbade her to love. Forbidden romance has never been so feathered.
films.pitch: Imagine if a Nature Doc show had a baby with a Forbidden Romance miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Ex-Spy.

films.title: Hard Sell: Maximum Commission
films.synopsis: Dale Pruitt sells industrial-grade laminating machines, and he is very good at it — which is precisely why a cartel of corporate spies wants his demo model. After a 90-mph freeway chase ends with his Buick wedged into a regional sales office, Dale realizes the safest place to hide is undercover... as the new guy in Cubicle 14B. Now he must dodge assassins, survive trust-fall exercises, and close the biggest deal of his life before Karen from Accounting figures out his badge is laminated wrong. He cries into a microwave Salisbury steak between every act. It's never been more dangerous to exceed quota.
films.pitch: Three-act structure: Car-Chase Thriller in act one, Workplace Comedy by act three, and a Traveling Salesman sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.

films.title: You Light Up My Apocalypse
films.synopsis: Eddie Marsh keeps the loneliest lighthouse on the coast, where his only companions are a temperamental foghorn and the unspeakable tentacled god slowly waking beneath the harbor. When chipper marine biologist Dawn rows ashore to study "unusual barnacles," Eddie discovers two terrifying truths: the Old One demands a human bride by the next full moon, and he has catastrophic, knee-buckling feelings for the woman it picked. Now he must woo Dawn, sabotage an eldritch wedding, and keep an entire town from being lovingly devoured. Turns out cosmic dread and first-date jitters feel exactly the same.
films.pitch: Set in a world where Romantic Comedy and Cosmic Horror coexist uneasily, our hero — a Lighthouse Keeper — must save... something. Probably a town.

films.title: Code Black: A Rock Opera of the Outer Dark
films.synopsis: Dr. Marguerite "Mags" Vane hasn't slept in nine days, hasn't blinked in three, and hasn't stopped singing since the thing on Table 4 woke up and harmonized back. When a patient flatlines into a non-Euclidean god, the only surgeon left in St. Abyssinia's Memorial must operate on a wound the size of the universe — using nothing but a scalpel, a power ballad, and a defibrillator that screams in a dead language. Each chest compression cracks reality wider. The chorus of the damned just wants her to hit the high note. It's a tragedy in three acts, four octaves, and infinite dread.
films.pitch: Cold open: a Burned-Out Surgeon, alone, in the rain. The genre? Cosmic Horror. The vibe? Unmistakably Rock Opera. Cue title card.

films.title: Disruptor: The Unicorn Sings Alone
films.synopsis: Tech billionaire Caspian Vale built an empire on a smart-mirror that tells you you're enough — and he no longer believes it. As his board stages a coup and his neural-implant startup leaks his own intrusive thoughts to investors, Caspian's grip on reality unravels into spontaneous, devastating renditions of 1980s power ballads sung to an empty boardroom. Every panic attack arrives as a key change; every betrayal cues the synths. A haunting, hilarious descent in which the only honest voice left is the karaoke machine.
films.pitch: It's Psychological Drama meets Jukebox Musical... but the protagonist is a Tech Billionaire.

films.title: The Ballad of Wendell Frost
films.synopsis: Wendell Frost was the most beloved fingerpicking folk troubadour in the Blue Ridge — until three rival banjoists vanished after sharing his campfire. A grief-rattled documentary crew follows Wendell as he records a haunting concept album about each disappearance, never noticing his lyrics describe details police never released. Part confessional ballad, part slow-tightening interrogation, the film watches a gentle man unravel one verse at a time. By the final track, you'll wonder whether he's mourning the missing — or auditioning their epitaphs.
films.pitch: It's Psychological Drama meets True Crime... but the protagonist is a Folk Musician.

films.title: The Last Draft at Dead Mule Gulch
films.synopsis: Reclusive novelist Cormac "Quill" Bellweather has spent eleven years in a sun-blasted desert shack, refusing to finish his masterpiece and shooting the doorknob off anyone who mentions deadlines. Then thirteen-year-old runaway Pearl shows up demanding to be his editor — and his apprentice gunslinger. As a vengeful publisher and his posse of bounty-hunting literary critics ride into town for the overdue manuscript, the grumpy hermit and the brave kid must finish the book, draw fast, and grow up together. One of them is forty years too old for a coming-of-age story. It does not go smoothly.
films.pitch: Imagine if a Spaghetti Western show had a baby with a Coming-of-Age miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Reclusive Author.

films.title: Officer Dingle and the Reasonable Mountain
films.synopsis: On his first day patrolling the kingdom-precinct of Glomthwaite, Rookie Constable Bartholomew Dingle is handed a cursed parking ticket, a sentient bicycle, and one impossible case: a mountain has filed a noise complaint against the entire town. As prophecy collides with paperwork, Dingle must arrest a sky that keeps apologizing, interrogate a duck who insists he's the rightful heir, and stop a wizard-developer from rezoning reality. He has no backup, no idea what's happening, and a soup that won't stop screaming in evidence. Saving the town would be easier if the town agreed it existed.
films.pitch: Set in a world where Surreal Comedy and High Fantasy coexist uneasily, our hero — a Rookie Cop — must save... something. Probably a town.

films.title: The Last Warlock of Burbank
films.synopsis: Three-time Best Actor nominee Roland Castavere, 71, hasn't had a hit since the Reagan administration and now does commercials for arthritis cream. So when literal interstellar beings land on his Malibu deck and announce that the universe's first ambassador must be its "greatest performer of heroic sorcery," Roland — who once played a wizard in a forgotten 1987 flop — bluffs his way into the role. Armed with a prop staff, a SAG card, and zero actual magic, he must broker peace with an alien empire that mistakes his line readings for divine incantations. Method acting has never had higher stakes.
films.pitch: It's First Contact meets Sword & Sorcery... but the protagonist is a Aging Movie Star.

films.title: The Salesman of Forgotten Wares
films.synopsis: 1888. Bartholomew Crick peddles thimbles, tonics, and pocket Bibles across the rain-drowned moors of northern England — until a flooded crossroads reveals that his sample case has been quietly selling pieces of forgotten gods. Now the old powers want their relics back, and they're willing to trade prophecy for a working pair of bootlaces. Cursed to never close a sale he doesn't regret, Bartholomew must out-haggle the Fates themselves before the last myth in his ledger comes due. A muddy, thunderstruck fable about commerce, faith, and a man who simply will not stop knocking.
films.pitch: Cold open: a Traveling Salesman, alone, in the rain. The genre? Period Piece. The vibe? Unmistakably Mythic Retelling. Cue title card.

films.title: Filibuster: Full Throttle for the Holidays
films.synopsis: Three weeks before a razor-thin Senate election, ruthless rising star Senator Margot Vance survives an armored-motorcade ambush and floors it into the snowbound town of Tinsel Hollow, where her only refuge is a cozy bed-and-breakfast run by a maddeningly cheerful widower named Caleb. Now she's doing 110 down icing roads while learning to bake gingerbread, dodging a black SUV that won't quit, and falling for the one man who has no idea who she is. Margot has always believed nothing matters more than winning — until a snowball fight, a stolen kiss, and a 200-mph candy-cane semi-truck pursuit make her reconsider her entire platform. Vote your heart. Buckle up.
films.pitch: Imagine if a Car-Chase Thriller show had a baby with a Holiday Romance miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Ambitious Politician.

films.title: Cold Cases of the Third Kind
films.synopsis: Mortician Edna Krell has buried this town's secrets for thirty years — so when alien dropships flatten the city, she treats the invasion like just another unsolved file gone cold. Armed with a corkboard, red yarn, and an embalmer's patience, Edna investigates each saucer attack as a "case," interviewing terrified survivors with the same flat monotone she uses on the bereaved. Her quirk: she insists every extraterrestrial deserves a proper death certificate. As the body count climbs and the ships keep landing, Edna realizes the only way to close the case is to interrogate the things that started it.
films.pitch: Picture a Alien Invasion setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Cold Case. Our reluctant hero: a Undertaker.

films.title: The Long Con at Coyote Flats
films.synopsis: Silver-tongued grifter Dallas "Three-Card" McRae rolls into the dying oil town of Coyote Flats planning to fleece one sucker and leave by sundown. Instead he uncovers a buried conspiracy involving a corrupt sheriff, a vanished water fortune, and a vault under the old saloon that nobody's cracked in forty years. The only way out alive is to assemble the worst crew in the county and pull the most elaborate heist the desert has ever seen — using nothing but lies, leverage, and a borrowed horse named Liability. Trouble is, every mark in town is running their own con right back at him.
films.pitch: A Con Artist stumbles into a Neo-Western conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Heist.

films.title: Abracadaver: A Trick Gone Medieval
films.synopsis: Las Vegas illusionist Reginald "The Astonishing Reg" Plonk botches his signature vanishing-box trick and accidentally yeets himself fourteen centuries into a kingdom that thinks his collapsible top hat is a holy relic. With nothing but a deck of marked cards, a chained dove, and crippling stage anxiety, Reg must defeat a genuine sorcerer who can do everything Reg only pretends to. Between battles he weeps quietly over microwave lasagna he smuggled through the time rift. To go home, he'll have to make the one thing disappear he's never managed before: an entire army.
films.pitch: Three-act structure: Time Travel in act one, Sword & Sorcery by act three, and a Stage Magician sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
